summary:
You start at index 0 of an array where each value is the farthest you can jump forward from that spot. Reaching the last index is always possible; your job is to find the fewest jumps needed to get there.

Return the minimum number of jumps required to arrive at the final index.

examples.explanation: Jump from index 0 to index 1 (1 step), then from index 1 to the last index (3 steps): 2 jumps total.
Index 0 to index 1, then index 1 to the end, for 2 jumps.

constraints: It is always possible to reach the last index
From index i you may jump forward up to nums[i] positions
